title: Vibrant Golden Fried Polish Sugar Crispy Flatbread
description: A traditional Polish flatbread, rich and crispy from the inside, with a sweet and sticky sugar lacquer-glaze on the outside.
instructions: Combine warm milk and yeast in a large mixing bowl. Let it sit for 5 minutes until frothy.
Add flour, sugar, salt, and egg to the mixing bowl. Mix well to form a dough.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover it with plastic wrap, and let it rise in a warm place for 1 hour.
Preheat a hot stone plate in the oven at 450°F (230°C) for 30 minutes.
Punch down the dough and divide it into 8 equal pieces.
Roll each piece into a ball and flatten it slightly into an oval shape.
Brush the top of each flatbread with melted butter and sprinkle with poppy seeds.
Transfer the flatbreads to the preheated stone plate and bake for 5-7 minutes or until golden brown.

While the flatbreads are baking, mix sugar and water in a small saucepan to make a sugar lacquer-glaze. Bring it to a boil, then reduce the heat to medium-low and simmer for 5 minutes until thickened.
Remove the flatbreads from the oven and brush them with the sugar lacquer-glaze.
